verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: That idols are not gods, and that the elements are not to be worshipped in the place of gods.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: In the cxiiith Psalm it is shown that “the idols of the heathen are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: They have a mouth, and speak not; eyes have they, and see not.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: They have ears, and hear not; neither is there any breath in their mouth.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Let those that make them be made like unto them.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Also in the Wisdom of Solomon: “They counted all the idols of the nations to be gods, which neither have the use of eyes to see, nor noses to draw breath, nor ears to hear, nor fingers on their hands to handle; and as for their feet, they are slow to go.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For man made them, and he that borrowed his own spirit fashioned them; but no man can make a god like unto himself.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: For, since he is mortal, he worketh a dead thing with wicked hands; for he himself is better than the things which he worshippeth, since he indeed lived once, but they never.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: In Exodus also: “Thou shalt not make to thee an idol, nor the likeness of anything.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Moreover, in Solomon, concerning the elements: “Neither by considering the works did they acknowledge who was the workmaster; but deemed either fire, or wind, or the swift air, or the circle of the stars, or the violent water, or the sun, or the moon, to be gods.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: On account of whose beauty, if they thought this, let them know how much more beautiful is the Lord than they.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Or if they admired their powers and operations, let them understand by them, that He that made these mighty things is mightier than they.”
